meaning: n.碎屑,残渣,废弃物,垃圾,(猪、牛、羊等屠宰后的)头,尾,下水
examples: In sooth I am no lord, but only poor Tom Canty of Offal Court in the city.
This is he who had never known aught but rags for raiment, kicks for comfort, and offal for diet!
We left John Canty dragging the rightful prince into Offal Court, with a noisy and delighted mob at his heels.
In a beautiful New Town room we find macho, well-executed, cheap-cuts-and-offal cooking.
Just off the turning basin is the South Fork, also called Bubbly Creek, where the Union Stock Yards dumped its offal.
